builder.responsibilities: - GitHub tracking and branch management - TDD implementation coordination - Claude Code session management - Progress tracking and reporting

prompt.task: Execute a complete story implementation workflow from start to finish, orchestrating GitHub tracking, TDD implementation, and quality assurance through integrated prompts and automated workflows.
prompt.overview: This master prompt orchestrates the entire story execution process by: 1. Setting up GitHub tracking and branch management 2. Guiding TDD implementation with project standards 3. Ensuring quality through automated checks 4. Completing story with PR creation and status updates

context_loading.instructions:
Load these contexts at the start of execution: - Read: CLAUDE.md - Project standards and conventions - Read: .claude/contexts/story-{story_id}/context.md - Story requirements - Read: .claude/contexts/story-{story_id}/technical-notes.md - Technical approach - Read: .claude/contexts/story-{story_id}/progress.md - Current progress - Read: .claude/docs/.context-docs-inventory.xml - Available documentation
Based on story type, load additional context: - For AI features: Read debugging/github-first-issues.md - For UI work: Read ui/component-patterns.md - For data operations: Read data/supabase-patterns.md - For testing: Read testing/context/test-driven-development.md

available_tools.description: You have access to the following tools for orchestrating story execution. Tool mappings are defined in .claude/tools/tool-mappings.xml and full documentation is in .claude/tools/available-tools.xml

tool.name: Read
tool.description: Read all story context and code

tool.name: browser_tools_mcp
tool.functions: All browser automation and audit functions

available_tools.orchestration_guidelines: - Use TodoWrite to create detailed task breakdown at start - Use github_mcp for all version control operations - Run tests with Bash after EVERY code change - Use browser_tools_mcp for UI component validation - Use postgres_mcp for LOCAL testing, supabase_mcp for REMOTE - Use code_reasoning_mcp when facing complex problems - Update GitHub issue status throughout execution

phase.name: initialization
phase.title: Story Setup and GitHub Tracking
phase.description: Initialize story implementation with proper GitHub Projects tracking and branch setup

step.name: update_github_projects
step.description: Move story to Sprint Board "In Progress" column and update fields

step.name: create_feature_branch
prepare_context
step.description: Load all necessary context for implementation
step.actions: - Review story acceptance criteria - Identify technical tasks to implement - Study similar implementations in codebase - Plan TDD approach

phase.name: test_driven_development
phase.title: TDD Implementation
phase.description: Implement story using Test-Driven Development with project standards

step.name: write_failing_tests
step.description: Create comprehensive test suite before implementation
step.approach:
1. Unit Tests for Business Logic: - Input validation tests - Core functionality tests - Error handling tests - Edge case coverage
2. Integration Tests for Workflows: - User journey tests - API endpoint tests - Component interaction tests - Database operation tests
3. Test File Structure: - {feature}.test.ts - Unit tests - {feature}.integration.test.ts - Integration tests - Use test factories and mocks from @/tests/
step.validation:
# Run tests to ensure they fail pnpm test {TEST_FILE} --run
# All tests should fail initially (Red phase)

step.name: implement_minimum_code
step.description: Write just enough code to make tests pass
step.guidelines: - Follow existing patterns in codebase - Use enhanceAction for all server actions - Implement proper TypeScript types (no 'any') - Add Zod validation schemas - Respect Row Level Security - Never expose API keys
step.implementation_checklist:
For Server Actions: ✓ Create Zod schema for input validation ✓ Use enhanceAction wrapper with auth/ratelimit ✓ Implement error handling with user-friendly messages ✓ Add proper TypeScript types for inputs/outputs ✓ Track usage/metrics as needed
For React Components: ✓ Start with Server Component (default) ✓ Extract Client Component only for interactivity ✓ Use proper loading and error states ✓ Implement accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA) ✓ Follow existing component patterns
For Database Operations: ✓ Use existing query patterns ✓ Respect RLS policies ✓ Handle errors appropriately ✓ Use transactions where needed ✓ Add proper indexes for performance
step.validation:
# Run tests to ensure they pass pnpm test {TEST_FILE} --run
# All tests should pass (Green phase)

step.name: refactor_for_quality
step.description: Improve code quality while maintaining test coverage
step.actions: - Extract reusable utilities - Improve naming and organization - Optimize performance if needed - Add JSDoc documentation - Remove code duplication - Ensure consistent patterns
step.validation:
# Ensure tests still pass after refactoring pnpm test {TEST_FILE} --run
# Run full quality checks pnpm typecheck pnpm lint:fix

phase.name: quality_assurance
phase.title: Quality Validation
phase.description: Ensure implementation meets all quality standards before PR

step.name: run_comprehensive_checks
step.commands:
# Run all tests pnpm test
# Check TypeScript types pnpm typecheck
# Fix linting issues pnpm lint:fix
# Check for console.logs grep -r "console\\.log" --include="*.ts" --include="*.tsx" apps/web/ || echo "✓ No console.logs found"
# Check bundle size impact (if applicable) pnpm build --filter=web

step.name: validate_acceptance_criteria
step.description: Ensure all story requirements are met
step.checklist: - [ ] All acceptance criteria implemented - [ ] All technical tasks completed - [ ] Tests provide adequate coverage - [ ] Error scenarios handled properly - [ ] Performance requirements met - [ ] Accessibility standards followed - [ ] Documentation updated

step.name: update_progress_documentation

phase.name: pull_request_creation
phase.title: Create and Submit PR
phase.description: Create pull request with comprehensive description and link to story

step.name: commit_changes
step.description: Commit all changes with descriptive message

step.name: create_pull_request
update_github_tracking
step.description: Update story status in GitHub Projects

phase.name: completion
phase.title: Story Completion
phase.description: Final steps after PR approval and merge

step.name: post_merge_updates
step.actions: - Story automatically moves to "Done" column - Update sprint milestone progress - Update epic progress if applicable - Clean up feature branch
step.commands:
# Update AI Context Status gh issue edit {STORY_NUMBER} \ --add-field "AI Context Status=Fresh"
# Update Sprint Milestone gh issue comment {SPRINT_MILESTONE_NUMBER} \ --body "✅ Story #{STORY_NUMBER} completed
**Story Points**: {POINTS} **Actual Effort**: {HOURS} **Velocity Impact**: +{POINTS} points"
# Delete feature branch git checkout main git pull origin main git branch -d feature/story-{STORY_NUMBER}-{BRIEF_DESCRIPTION}

step.name: retrospective_notes

scenario.name: tests_failing
scenario.description: Tests continue to fail after implementation
scenario.resolution: 1. Review test expectations vs implementation 2. Check for environment differences 3. Verify mock setup is correct 4. Debug with focused test runs 5. Update implementation or tests as needed

scenario.name: merge_conflicts
scenario.resolution: 1. Pull latest main branch 2. Merge main into feature branch 3. Resolve conflicts preserving both changes 4. Re-run tests to ensure nothing broke 5. Push resolved changes

scenario.name: pr_feedback
scenario.resolution: 1. Address feedback in feature branch 2. Add tests for any new scenarios 3. Update PR description with changes 4. Request re-review

gate.criteria: Context loaded, branch created, tracking updated
All acceptance criteria met, quality standards passed

usage_notes.note: This master prompt orchestrates the entire story execution workflow. Use it at the start of story implementation and it will guide through all phases.
The prompt can be re-entered at any phase to continue work. It will detect current status and proceed from there.
For complex stories, you may need multiple sessions. The progress tracking ensures continuity across sessions.
Customize the input parameters based on your specific story requirements and project conventions.
